steps.title: Identify the crucial qualifier
steps.content: The passage explains the approach's underutilization: “archival specimens are sometimes stored in ways that compromise DNA quality.” The word sometimes is crucial—it signals the problem is selective, affecting certain specimens rather than all.

steps.title: Match the logic to the choices
steps.content: D states that specimens “may contain valuable genomic data” while “not all of them can yield usable hDNA,” capturing both the passage's acknowledgment of benefits and the variable quality from storage issues. A contradicts the passage's praise for the approach, B adds unsupported claims about extraction timelines, and C misidentifies their primary use. That matches choice D — D is correct.
